The plant is native to tropical Africa, and its classification is as follows: Neorautanenia seineri is classified as a flowering plant, specifically a legume. It is further classified as a species within the subfamily Crotalaridoideae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the Fabaceae family.

intro: Neorautanenia seineri, also known as Kaffir potato or devil's cotton, is a perennial legume native to Southern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its reported health benefits of reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Neorautanenia seineri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
